jokes.text: Oh, 'cause I put a bunch of rocks in 'em.
jokes.setup: Someone asks why urinals are filled with rocks, expecting a logical explanation
jokes.punchline: Charlie's matter-of-fact delivery of completely illogical reasoning
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie logic - completely absurd but delivered with total confidence. Strong character integration as only Charlie would think this makes sense.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Oh, 'cause I put a bunch of rocks in 'em.

jokes.text: Well, you know how they were broken, so I was, like, filling them with ice. Now the ice machine's broken, so I'm just filling them up with rocks.
jokes.punchline: The complete breakdown of any semblance of logic - rocks replacing ice in urinals
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of absurdist logic. The pseudo-reasoning makes it even funnier - Charlie genuinely thinks this chain of substitutions makes sense.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Well, you know how they were broken, so I was, like, filling them with ice. Now the ice machine's broken, so I'm just filling them up with rocks.

jokes.text: Oh, something just dripped in my mouth!
jokes.punchline: The disgusting reality of something unknown dripping into his mouth
jokes.explanation: Basic gross-out humor that relies on disgust rather than wit, but serves the bar's decrepit state

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Oh, something just dripped in my mouth!

jokes.text: What'd it taste like?
jokes.setup: Mac is disgusted by mystery liquid in his mouth
jokes.punchline: Frank's inappropriate curiosity about the taste
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frank moment - completely inappropriate response that shows his warped priorities and lack of empathy

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: What'd it taste like?

jokes.text: Who gives a shit what it tasted like, Frank?
jokes.punchline: Mac's exasperated reaction highlighting Frank's weirdness
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man reaction that emphasizes the absurdity of Frank's question

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Who gives a shit what it tasted like, Frank?

jokes.text: What's up, bitches?
jokes.punchline: Her overconfident attitude given her typical loser status
jokes.explanation: Standard Dee overconfidence that will inevitably backfire, setting up her comeuppance

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: What's up, bitches?

jokes.text: My surrogate money came in. 30 G's, babies.
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to profiting from surrogacy scheme
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about commodifying pregnancy, perfectly fits Dee's amoral character

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: My surrogate money came in. 30 G's, babies.

jokes.text: What the hell are you talking about? You did nothing for nine months.
jokes.setup: Gang demands cut of surrogate money
jokes.punchline: Dee's dismissive response ignoring they literally did nothing
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Dee's selfishness, though the joke itself is straightforward

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: What the hell are you talking about? You did nothing for nine months.

jokes.text: Okay, I made all the contacts, I pushed that little monkey out and now I'm going to be claiming him as a dependent and getting tax breaks for the next 18 years.
jokes.setup: Dee explains her role in the scheme
jokes.punchline: Referring to baby as 'monkey' and planning long-term tax fraud
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation showing the full scope of Dee's scheme. 'Little monkey' is a perfect dehumanizing touch.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Okay, I made all the contacts, I pushed that little monkey out and now I'm going to be claiming him as a dependent and getting tax breaks for the next 18 years.

jokes.text: Uh, you guys all better eat a dick, 'cause Sweet Dee beat the system.
jokes.punchline: Vulgar victory declaration that will obviously backfire
jokes.explanation: Classic setup for Dee's inevitable downfall. The crude language fits character but joke relies on dramatic irony.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Uh, you guys all better eat a dick, 'cause Sweet Dee beat the system.

jokes.text: Whatever god you're selling, I ain't buying it.
jokes.setup: Dee assumes visitor is religious solicitor
jokes.punchline: Completely wrong assumption about IRS agent
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that shows Dee's assumptions and sets up the audit reveal

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Whatever god you're selling, I ain't buying it.

jokes.text: Letters? What is it, 1986? I don't do letters, lady. I'm all digital.
jokes.punchline: Dee's pretentious dismissal of mail as outdated
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Dee's delusional self-importance and tech-savviness while avoiding responsibility

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Letters? What is it, 1986? I don't do letters, lady. I'm all digital.

jokes.text: Your license plate says '$CAMMIN.'
jokes.punchline: Her license plate literally advertises scamming
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual gag that perfectly encapsulates Dee's stupidity and arrogance. The contrast between her denial and obvious evidence is perfect.

dialogue.speaker: IRS Agent
dialogue.line: Your license plate says '$CAMMIN.'

jokes.text: Uh, no. What do you mean no? Yes.
jokes.setup: Agent confronts Dee about license plate
jokes.punchline: Dee's pathetic attempt to deny undeniable evidence
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Dee's inability to handle being caught red-handed

dialogue.speaker: IRS Agent
dialogue.line: Uh, no.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: What do you mean no?

dialogue.speaker: IRS Agent
dialogue.line: Yes.

jokes.text: But now's not a great time, you know what I mean? Because I left him in the car-- with the window down of course.
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions leaving baby in car like it's responsible
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about child endangerment delivered with Dee's characteristic cluelessness about proper parenting

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: But now's not a great time, you know what I mean? Because I left him in the car-- with the window down of course.

jokes.text: Because you're sleazy and we don't trust you.
jokes.punchline: Blunt honesty about Frank's character
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective character humor - stating the obvious about Frank with casual directness

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Because you're sleazy and we don't trust you.

jokes.text: Emotion will be suppressed and reason will prevail. Reason will prevail!
jokes.punchline: Mac immediately and emotionally chants the catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Sets up excellent running gag about their inability to be rational. The immediate contradiction is perfect.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Emotion will be suppressed and reason will prevail. Reason will prevail!

jokes.text: You don't have to say it right now, because I'm just explaining to Frank that reason will prevail... Reason will prevail!
jokes.setup: Dennis tries to control when they use catchphrase
jokes.punchline: Mac can't help himself and says it again
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of the running gag showing Mac's inability to control himself despite their rational goals

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: You don't have to say it right now, because I'm just explaining to Frank that reason will prevail...

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Reason will prevail!

jokes.text: Like one that comes to mind is the dead dog. What dead dog? The dead dog in the alley.
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation of dead dog everyone forgot about
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor setup that casually introduces disturbing element. Perfect Charlie priorities.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Like one that comes to mind is the dead dog.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: What dead dog?

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: The dead dog in the alley.

jokes.text: Okay... Uh, okay, sometimes dogs die.
jokes.setup: Mac tries to make rational argument
jokes.punchline: Immediately starts with obvious, useless statement
jokes.explanation: Perfect start to Mac's spectacular failure at rational discourse - beginning with the most obvious possible statement

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Okay... Uh, okay, sometimes dogs die.

jokes.text: I don't know how to express myself unless through anger and personal attack!
jokes.setup: Mac's rational argument completely falls apart
jokes.punchline: Perfect self-awareness about his communication limitations
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta moment where character explicitly states the show's comedy formula. Self-aware and perfectly timed emotional breakdown.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I don't know how to express myself unless through anger and personal attack!

jokes.text: I want a dog funeral!
jokes.punchline: Charlie's emotional outburst about his specific desire
jokes.explanation: Perfect emotional culmination showing Charlie's strange priorities and genuine feelings about proper dog burial

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I want a dog funeral!

jokes.text: But that dog is turning into hot soup out there in the sun.
jokes.setup: Dennis makes practical argument about compromise
jokes.punchline: Disgusting but vivid description of decomposing dog
jokes.explanation: Excellent gross-out imagery that's both funny and motivating for urgency. 'Hot soup' is perfectly disgusting word choice.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: But that dog is turning into hot soup out there in the sun.

jokes.text: Thick or thin?
jokes.setup: Dennis mentions lime-cutting as next agenda item
jokes.punchline: The absurd specificity of what they're debating
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the lime debate - shows how they focus on trivial matters while bigger issues exist

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Thick or thin?

jokes.text: What the hell?! Thin limes?! People will choke! People will die!
jokes.punchline: Charlie's dramatic overreaction treating it as life-or-death
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation showing Charlie's bizarre passionate attachment to thick limes as a safety issue

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: What the hell?! Thin limes?! People will choke! People will die!

jokes.text: I'm going to put my thumb through your eye, you little bitch!
jokes.punchline: Extreme violent threat over produce preparation
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation showing how quickly their 'rational' system breaks down into violence over nothing

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I'm going to put my thumb through your eye, you little bitch!

jokes.text: That's two votes against two votes. A perfect example of when democracy has failed.
jokes.setup: Vote on limes ends in tie
jokes.punchline: Dennis immediately declares democracy failed at first sign of difficulty
jokes.explanation: Brilliant political satire about giving up on democracy immediately when it doesn't work perfectly. Great Dennis character moment.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: That's two votes against two votes. A perfect example of when democracy has failed.

jokes.text: Are you having another baby?
jokes.punchline: Assumes she's pregnant again rather than in trouble
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Mac's assumption Dee's only source of money is pregnancy schemes

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Are you having another baby?

jokes.text: I just got to find a baby by 3:00 in the afternoon.
jokes.punchline: Casually treating finding a baby like running an errand
jokes.explanation: Perfectly dark and absurd - treating baby acquisition like a time-sensitive shopping trip

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: I just got to find a baby by 3:00 in the afternoon.

jokes.text: How many babies you need? Yeah. Just the one baby. How long? Couple hours?
jokes.setup: Frank offers to get Dee a baby
jokes.punchline: Treating baby rental like a business transaction
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor treating baby procurement like equipment rental. Frank's businesslike approach is perfect.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: How many babies you need?

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Yeah. Just the one baby.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: How long? Couple hours?

jokes.text: You want it for life or you...? Just a short-time baby.
jokes.punchline: The concept of 'short-time baby' like it's a car rental
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of the baby rental concept. 'Short-time baby' is brilliantly absurd phrasing that sounds like business terminology.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: You want it for life or you...? Just a short-time baby.

jokes.text: We are now the Pickle Party.
jokes.punchline: Completely arbitrary party name that solves nothing
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist political commentary - forming arbitrary party to ensure victory rather than compromise

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: We are now the Pickle Party.

jokes.text: Give me a beer with a pickle in it.
jokes.punchline: Immediately orders disgusting drink to prove point
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie moment - actually wanting to drink the gross thing he's advocating for

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Give me a beer with a pickle in it.

jokes.text: If you're not as educated or as informed, what you do is you start your own party and you yell the loudest.
jokes.punchline: Charlie perfectly describes modern political tactics
jokes.explanation: Brilliant political satire that's both accurate and self-aware. Charlie inadvertently describes real political strategy.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: If you're not as educated or as informed, what you do is you start your own party and you yell the loudest.

jokes.text: Reason will prevail. / Pickles will prevail!
jokes.punchline: Charlie corrupting it with their pickle agenda
jokes.explanation: Good callback transformation showing how their system corrupts.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Reason will prevail.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Pickles will prevail!

jokes.text: Big. It should be the centerpiece of the whole room. I mean, it should dominate every conversation.
jokes.punchline: Charlie's vision of conversation-dominating religious imagery
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Charlie's extreme religious vision - wanting intimidating religious presence

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Big. It should be the centerpiece of the whole room. I mean, it should dominate every conversation.

jokes.text: That's gonna be terrifying! It should be terrifying. That's how you know God loves you, Charlie.
jokes.punchline: Charlie's warped theology about divine love through terror
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark religious humor showing Charlie's twisted understanding of Christianity - fear equals love

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: That's gonna be terrifying!

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: It should be terrifying. That's how you know God loves you, Charlie.

jokes.text: You're a piece of shit and you're ugly and you ooze sleaze and you're very, very ugly.
jokes.setup: Dennis explains why Frank needs him
jokes.punchline: Brutal honesty delivered matter-of-factly, emphasizing ugly twice
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Dennis's casual cruelty and the repetition of 'ugly' adds emphasis

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: You're a piece of shit and you're ugly and you ooze sleaze and you're very, very ugly.

jokes.text: Wolf Cola, that's a bogus soda distributor. I made it up. It's fake!
jokes.setup: Dennis asks about Wolf Cola in Frank's books
jokes.punchline: Frank proudly admits to completely made-up business
jokes.explanation: Great setup for Wolf Cola running gag. Frank's pride in his fake business is perfect character moment.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Wolf Cola, that's a bogus soda distributor. I made it up. It's fake!

jokes.text: He's gotta be drenched in blood. I mean, covered in blood.
jokes.punchline: Charlie's insistence on maximum gore for religious display
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Charlie's extreme religious vision, though somewhat repetitive of earlier beat

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: He's gotta be drenched in blood. I mean, covered in blood.

jokes.text: You're sweating profusely.
jokes.setup: Dee nervously waits for fake baby
jokes.punchline: Agent notices obvious signs of Dee's panic
jokes.explanation: Basic visual comedy showing Dee's obvious nervousness, functional but not particularly clever

dialogue.speaker: IRS Agent
dialogue.line: You're sweating profusely.

jokes.text: Crying baby! Crying baby here!
jokes.setup: Dee desperately needs baby for audit
jokes.punchline: Gang's terrible fake baby sounds from outside
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing their amateur attempt at baby deception with obvious fake sounds

dialogue.line: Crying baby! Crying baby here!

jokes.text: Do you really want a kidnapping on top of all the other shit you got going on right now?
jokes.punchline: Mac casually references kidnapping as logical next step
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor escalation - treating kidnapping as a practical concern rather than moral issue

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Do you really want a kidnapping on top of all the other shit you got going on right now?

jokes.text: Very, very sick baby. Very sick baby!
jokes.setup: Dee tries to explain baby absence
jokes.punchline: Claiming baby is too sick to show, selling the lie harder
jokes.explanation: Decent cringe comedy showing Dee digging deeper into lies with fake concern for nonexistent baby

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Very, very sick baby. Very sick baby!

jokes.text: I like to call it my, uh, my God hole.
jokes.punchline: Calling existential void his 'God hole' with sexual undertones
jokes.explanation: Excellent Dennis character moment combining pseudo-intellectual philosophy with sexual innuendo. Perfectly absurd terminology.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I like to call it my, uh, my God hole.

jokes.text: What you really need to be doing is convincing the IRS that Dee's baby is dead.
jokes.setup: Gang discusses how to help Dee
jokes.punchline: Dennis's solution is to fake baby's death
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark escalation that's both logically sound within their world and morally horrifying. Perfect Dennis solution.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: What you really need to be doing is convincing the IRS that Dee's baby is dead.

jokes.text: This is dark. Darkest thing we've ever done.
jokes.punchline: Characters acknowledge they've reached new moral low
jokes.explanation: Good meta moment where characters acknowledge crossing new moral boundary, adding self-awareness to dark humor

dialogue.line: This is dark. Darkest thing we've ever done.

jokes.text: Baby croaked.
jokes.setup: IRS agent arrives for fake funeral
jokes.punchline: Casual, crude announcement of baby's 'death'
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark economy - delivering tragic news with slang term 'croaked' shows their complete lack of appropriate emotion

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Baby croaked.

jokes.text: I can't cry on command. / Jesus Christ, Dee. I mean, how are you planning on becoming an actress at all? You can't make people laugh, 'cause you're not funny, you can't cry on command. What can you do?
jokes.setup: Dee's inability to cry for performance
jokes.punchline: Dennis's brutal catalog of Dee's complete lack of talent
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis cruelty disguised as logic, systematically destroying Dee's dreams.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: I can't cry on command.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Jesus Christ, Dee. I mean, how are you planning on becoming an actress at all? You can't make people laugh, 'cause you're not funny, you can't cry on command. What can you do?

jokes.text: At times like this, I-I like to quench my thirst with a delicious Wolf Cola.
jokes.punchline: Dennis uses tragedy as product placement opportunity
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback and escalation - turning fake funeral into advertising opportunity for fake product. Peak Dennis narcissism.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: At times like this, I-I like to quench my thirst with a delicious Wolf Cola.

jokes.text: There's nothing like that... to wash away the sorrow.
jokes.punchline: Claims soda literally washes away grief
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of inappropriate product placement - connecting soda consumption to emotional healing

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: There's nothing like that... to wash away the sorrow.

jokes.text: You were my special angel. And only angels can fly.
jokes.setup: Dee gives eulogy for fake baby
jokes.punchline: Bad poetry that makes no sense for baby's death
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Dee's bad acting and inappropriate eulogy that doesn't make logical sense

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: You were my special angel. And only angels can fly.

jokes.text: Are my eyes bleeding? Oh, my God, is there blood?! You son of a bitch!
jokes.setup: Dennis used chili powder on Dee's eyes
jokes.punchline: Dee's panic about potential eye damage
jokes.explanation: Payoff to chili powder setup, physical comedy but fairly predictable.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Are my eyes bleeding? Oh, my God, is there blood?! You son of a bitch!

jokes.text: Before you could walk. Before you could talk. And before you had the chance to get together the proper paperwork
jokes.punchline: Accidentally reveals the real purpose of the fake death
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation where Charlie accidentally reveals their scheme while trying to be poetic. Perfect character mistake.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Before you could walk. Before you could talk. And before you had the chance to get together the proper paperwork

jokes.text: Wolf Cola, everyone. It's the right cola for closure.
jokes.punchline: Dennis creates advertising slogan connecting soda to grieving process
jokes.explanation: Peak absurdist humor - creating perfect advertising slogan for inappropriate moment. 'Right cola for closure' is brilliantly specific.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Wolf Cola, everyone. It's the right cola for closure.

jokes.text: Prepare for your hearts to turn to stones!
jokes.punchline: Dramatic biblical language for revealing dead dog
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic escalation with biblical language that's completely overblown for the reveal of dead dog

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Prepare for your hearts to turn to stones!

jokes.text: It's the dead dog from the alleyway. I was compromising.
jokes.punchline: Charlie's matter-of-fact explanation of his 'compromise'
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that ties earlier dead dog debate to current crisis. Charlie's logic is characteristically twisted but consistent.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: It's the dead dog from the alleyway. I was compromising.

jokes.text: all in favor of going back to the old system of organized chaos, where emotion trumps reason every single time and we just yell at each other to get the shit that we want, raise your hands.
jokes.punchline: Dennis formally votes to return to their dysfunctional normal
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta ending that explicitly describes the show's comedy formula while acknowledging their democratic experiment's failure.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: all in favor of going back to the old system of organized chaos, where emotion trumps reason every single time and we just yell at each other to get the shit that we want, raise your hands.
